response: {"purpose":"This figure demonstrates how eCLIP's paired size-matched input (SMInput) normalization strategy enables accurate, RNA-centric identification of protein binding to highly abundant noncoding RNAs, using RBFOX2 and the lincRNA MALAT1 as key examples. Panels (a) and (b) show that comparing eCLIP signal against SMInput markedly improves the enrichment specificity of CLIPper-identified clusters across diverse genomic regions in 102 eCLIP experiments conducted in K562 and HepG2 cells. Panels (c) and (d) highlight that different RNA-binding proteins (RBPs) display distinct and reproducible binding patterns along MALAT1, as evidenced by fold-enrichment values and read density tracks across 204 datasets. This matters because abundant noncoding RNAs like MALAT1 are prone to non-specific signal in conventional CLIP methods, and demonstrating that eCLIP can resolve specific, biologically meaningful RBP binding at these loci validates its utility for studying noncoding RNA regulation.","title":"eCLIP Identifies Protein Binding to Abundant Noncoding RNAs"}
